summary: Workflow + module demonstrate excellent PowerShell practices (Set-StrictMode, parameter validation, explicit error handling, least-privilege permissions, pinned actions). Provider pattern enables testable, hermetic design. No bloat or duplication. However: (1) only supports package.json despite task mentioning "requirements.txt or similar"; (2) fixture files (license-policy.json, license-database.json, manifest.json) referenced in workflow not provided—cannot verify workflow runs under `act` as required; (3) tight coupling to NPM manifest structure limits reuse; (4) minimal defensive checks (e.g., policy object lacks keys). Testability is strong but actual test file absent, preventing confirmation that "ALL TESTS MUST RUN THROUGH ACT". Code quality for deliverable scope is solid; execution verification blocked.
